commit.message:
perf(crm): split cab/det queries para evitar timeout en Turso

Problema: queries con COUNT(DISTINCT num_doc) + SUM(pvp*unidades) +
LIKE + SUBSTR sobre 34K filas/mes hacían timeout en Vercel/Turso.

Solución: separar cada tabla en queries simples cab + det:
- Cabeceras (es_cabecera=1): COUNT(*) para tickets (no DISTINCT)
- Detalle (es_cabecera=0): SUM(pvp*unidades) para facturación
- Combinar resultados en JS antes de INSERT

Cada query individual filtra con es_cabecera en WHERE → usa índice
(fecha, es_cabecera) para ir directo a ~16K filas en vez de 34K.

Nuevo índice: ventas(es_cabecera, fecha, tipo) para cubrir el filtro
tipo IN ('Contado','Credito') en queries de cabecera.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: apply _sanitizeFlowXml in _regroupFlowElements (deploy-time cleanup)

The previous fix applied the sanitizer on cache hits and during generation,
but existing job records already have the invalid XML stored in
generated_metadata. Since _regroupFlowElements is called at every deploy
(both in deploymentService and remediationService), applying _sanitizeFlowXml
there ensures all flow XML is cleaned before it reaches Salesforce regardless
of when the job was generated or cached.

commit.message:
docs: remove ETS hot cache from TS spec

At 30K samples/sec per series, duplicating every write to ETS is pure
overhead. The columnar file's open partition already has raw bytes
readable via pread (~50μs). Drop HOT_WINDOW parameter, simplify to
three-tier model (warm cache + NVMe + expired).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: os.path.join supports up to 8 path components (was 4)

CPython's os.path.join accepts *paths (unlimited). Molt's AOT stub had
only 4 named params (first through fourth), causing TypeError when
click calls join() with 5+ args. Extended to 8 params which covers
all practical usage in the ecosystem (click, flask, django, etc.).

commit.message:
fix: apply Flow XML sanitizer on cache hits in generateMergedFlow

The post-processor that strips invalid elements (<label> in textTemplates,
<apexPluginCalls>, <connectors>, <locationX/Y> in textTemplates, <label> in
formulas) was only applied after fresh LLM generation. Cache hits returned
the raw cached XML, which may have been stored before these post-processors
were introduced — causing Salesforce deployment failures for orgs that share
the same source automations as previously-processed orgs.

Fix: extract the stripping logic into _sanitizeFlowXml() and call it both
after fresh generation (replacing the inline code) and on cache hits, so
old cache entries are automatically upgraded without a cache bust.

fix(crm): corregir facturación — usar SUM(pvp*unidades) en vez de SUM(imp_neto)

Bug: el precálculo usaba SUM(ABS(imp_neto)) en cabeceras, que daba ~37%
del importe real. La fórmula correcta (idéntica a queries.ts) es:
  - Facturación = SUM(pvp * unidades) en líneas de detalle
  - Tickets = COUNT(DISTINCT num_doc)
  - Filtro: tipo IN (Contado, Credito), excluir docs W, anuladas, traspasos

Cambios:
- precalcular/route.ts: reescribir las 4 queries INSERT con FACT_WHERE
  y SUM(pvp * unidades). Añadir 5ª query para receta vs libre.
- crm-queries.ts: getCrmResumen calcula pct_receta desde segmentación.
  getCrmSegmentacion lee byReceta real (__receta__/__libre__).
  getCrmComparativa incluye 2024 además de 2025/2026.

Después de deploy: POST /api/crm/precalcular?year=2024/2025/2026

commit.message:
fix: add init to mock_outputs_allowed_terraform_commands in all envs

Terragrunt resolves dependency outputs during init, not just plan.
With unapplied dependencies (first deploy), init would fail with
"detected no outputs" for key-vault and postgresql roots that depend
on aks. Adding "init" allows mock outputs to satisfy the dependency
resolver during the init phase of run-all apply.

commit.message:
fix: c_api test suite — prevent panic recursion + stack overflow

Two fixes to the c_api test infrastructure:

1. GIL catch_unwind guard: when a panic originates from the allocation
   system (use-after-free, invalid type_id, double free), skip the
   raise_exception call that would re-enter the corrupted allocator
   and cause infinite recursion via catch_unwind → raise → panic → ...

2. c_api_mapping_getitemstring: run on a dedicated 8MB thread to prevent
   stack overflow from deep GIL re-entry (3+ nested with_gil_entry!
   frames accumulate when prior tests consume the main thread's stack).

Results: 61 c_api tests pass (was: SIGABRT after 22). The remaining
3-4 failures cascade from attr_object_ic_keeps_type_objects_distinct
which fails when molt_type_new returns None due to prior test state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix context starvation: 200K fixed overhead → 20% dynamic margin

Root cause: the agent reserved a fixed 200K tokens for "tools + template +
safety", leaving only 45K usable tokens (17.5%) on a 262K context model.
File content was evicted after 2-3 tool calls, causing the model to loop
trying to re-read files it could no longer see.

Changes:
- Replace fixed 200K overhead with 20% of context_length (scales properly)
- ContextMap now uses max_context_tokens (193K) not max_tokens (16K)
- ContextCompressor threshold now based on conversation budget, not output
- token_budget defaults to 60% of context_length when not explicit

Before: max_context_tokens=45,760 (17.5%), compression at 12K tokens
After:  max_context_tokens=193,332 (73.8%), compression at 145K tokens

Tested: selfware now builds a 685-line website AND verifies it in one
shot without any re-read loops. Previously failed every time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: selfware
repository.description: Your personal AI workshop — software you own, software that lasts

commit.message:
fix: SSA undef for loop phis + TIR loop-aware roundtrip groundwork

Phase 2 progress on TIR loop support:

1. SSA converter: collect_branch_args now uses undef_value for
   variables not yet defined on the current path (loop entry edges).
   This eliminates the "unresolved SSA branch arg" panic for loop
   headers. The undef value is correct SSA semantics — on the first
   iteration, loop-body variables are undefined.

2. lower_to_simple: emits loop_start (LoopHeader), loop_break_if_true
   (CondBranch in header), loop_continue + loop_end (LoopEnd Branch)
   based on LoopRole metadata. This is the structural marker
   reconstruction the native backend needs.

3. Loop functions still preserve original ops because the native
   backend's iter_next peephole optimizer requires the specific
   op ordering (iter_next → index(pair,1) → loop_break_if_true →
   index(pair,0)) that TIR block linearization doesn't yet preserve.

Remaining TODO for full loop TIR: teach lower_to_simple to emit
the complete for-loop peephole pattern inline within the loop_start/
loop_end region, rather than as separate blocks with labels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Move email/header store to ~/.local/share; rename cache → local store

Messages are persistent user data, not disposable cache. Moving them to
XDG_DATA_HOME (~/.local/share) protects them from cache-cleaning tools.

- platform_data_dir(): new platform function (XDG_DATA_HOME / ~/.local/share)
- cache_store.{h,c} renamed to local_store.{h,c}
- cache_exists/save/load → local_msg_exists/save/load
- hcache_exists/save/load/evict_stale → local_hdr_exists/save/load/evict_stale
- ui.ini moved from ~/.cache to ~/.local/share
- test_cache.c → test_local_store.c with corrected paths and pre-clean

New layout:
  ~/.local/share/email-cli/messages/<folder>/<uid>.eml  (full messages)
  ~/.local/share/email-cli/headers/<folder>/<uid>.hdr   (header copies)
  ~/.local/share/email-cli/ui.ini                       (UI preferences)
  ~/.cache/email-cli/logs/                              (logs, unchanged)

commit.message:
fix: evict cached locals for names declared global in nested functions

When a FunctionDef at module scope declares `global x`, the module-level
cached local for `x` must be evicted so subsequent reads go through
module_get_attr. Otherwise the stale SSA value from the initial
assignment shadows the mutation made by the called function.

commit.message:
fix: TIR loop-aware SSA scope boundary + proper roundtrip groundwork

The TIR SSA converter does not yet implement the Braun et al. (2013)
incomplete-phi protocol for loop headers. Loop-carried values cause
"unresolved SSA branch arg" panics, and lower_to_simple cannot yet
reconstruct the structural loop markers (loop_start, loop_end,
loop_break_if_true, loop_continue) that the native backend requires.

This commit:
1. Preserves original ops for loop-bearing functions (has loop_start
   or for_iter ops). Non-loop functions still get TIR optimization.
2. Adds LoopRole-aware emission in lower_to_simple as groundwork:
   - loop_start before LoopHeader blocks
   - loop_break_if_true for CondBranch in loop headers
   - loop_continue + loop_end for LoopEnd Branch terminators
3. Restricts annotate_type_flags to known arithmetic/comparison ops
   to prevent the type refiner from incorrectly marking iter/list
   results as fast_int.

Phase 2 TODO: implement proper loop-carried phi insertion using
sealed/unsealed blocks, then enable TIR for loop functions.

Verified: for, while, comprehension, range, nested loops, recursion,
try/except, list +=, dict, string methods, dynamic kwargs all pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: return signature mismatch — void functions must emit return_(&[]) not return_(&[value])

Four call sites in function_compiler.rs emitted return_(&[err_val]) or
return_(&[none_bits]) unconditionally, regardless of whether the function
signature includes a return type. For void-returning functions (has_ret=false),
this violated the Cranelift verifier: "arguments of return must match
function signature". The verifier error caused the function to be replaced
with a trap stub, producing SIGILL at runtime.

Affected paths:
- Recursion guard error block (direct call fast path)
- Exception propagation return block (check_exception handler)
- Recursion guard failure in then_fail_block
- Recursion guard failure in else_fail_block

Fix: check has_ret before emitting return — void functions use return_(&[]).

This was the root cause of ALL programs crashing with SIGILL after the
void-return signature separation in commit 13f88875.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reduce auto-backfill batch size from 25 to 10

means filers past position 10 never get reached. Reducing to 10 ensures
all filers in the batch have a chance to be attempted, and large filers
like Kate Brown don't get perpetually buried.
